one_time_description: Subsequent to year-end, the First Nation received their share of the Robinson Huron Treaty settlement from the Government of Canada and Province of Ontario in the amount of $234,317,862, of which $131,255,000 was distributed to eligible members.
economic_notes: First Nation has 14.28% equity interest in UCCMLP (building material supply) and 16.66% equity interest in MMPLP (power limited partnership), both accounted for under the modified equity method. Robinson Huron Treaty settlement of $234,317,862 received subsequent to year-end.
notes: Fiscal year 2022 (ended March 31, 2022). Two government business enterprises accounted for by modified equity method: UCCMLP and MMPLP. Major subsequent event: Robinson Huron Treaty settlement of $234,317,862 received after year-end, with $131,255,000 distributed to members - this is not reflected in the 2022 financial statements but is flagged as a significant subsequent event. Note that the audit report is dated April 11, 2025, which is unusually late relative to the March 31, 2022 year-end. Housing expenses combined Social Housing ($89,597) and Housing and Community Property ($400,788) = $490,385. Employment and Economic Development expenses of $130,803 not mapped to a standard category. No schedule of remuneration provided. OSR calculated as total revenue ($5,239,014) minus total government transfers - Provincial ($863,422) minus Federal ($2,824,661) = $1,550,931.
